system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

Conversation started: Thursday, May 07, 2026 01:24 PM
Model: deepseek-chat
Provider: custom

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. Do NOT emit MEDIA:/path tags (those are only intercepted on messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.; on the CLI they render as literal text). When referring to a file you created or changed, just state its absolute path in plain text; the user can open it from there.

function.name: browser_back
function.description: Navigate back to the previous page in browser history. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_click
function.description: Click on an element identified by its ref ID from the snapshot (e.g., '@e5'). The ref IDs are shown in square brackets in the snapshot output. Requires browser_navigate and browser_snapshot to be called first.

ref.description: The element reference from the snapshot (e.g., '@e5', '@e12')

function.name: browser_console
function.description: Get browser console output and JavaScript errors from the current page. Returns console.log/warn/error/info messages and uncaught JS exceptions. Use this to detect silent JavaScript errors, failed API calls, and application warnings. Requires browser_navigate to be called first. When 'expression' is provided, evaluates JavaScript in the page context and returns the result — use this for DOM inspection, reading page state, or extracting data programmatically.

clear.description: If true, clear the message buffers after reading

expression.description: JavaScript expression to evaluate in the page context. Runs in the browser like DevTools console — full access to DOM, window, document. Return values are serialized to JSON. Example: 'document.title' or 'document.querySelectorAll("a").length'

function.name: browser_get_images
function.description: Get a list of all images on the current page with their URLs and alt text. Useful for finding images to analyze with the vision tool. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_navigate
function.description: Navigate to a URL in the browser. Initializes the session and loads the page. Must be called before other browser tools. For simple information retrieval, prefer web_search or web_extract (faster, cheaper). For plain-text endpoints — URLs ending in .md, .txt, .json, .yaml, .yml, .csv, .xml, raw.githubusercontent.com, or any documented API endpoint — prefer curl via the terminal tool or web_extract; the browser stack is overkill and much slower for these. Use browser tools when you need to interact with a page (click, fill forms, dynamic content). Returns a compact page snapshot with interactive elements and ref IDs — no need to call browser_snapshot separately after navigating.

function.name: browser_press
function.description: Press a keyboard key. Useful for submitting forms (Enter), navigating (Tab), or keyboard shortcuts. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_scroll
function.description: Scroll the page in a direction. Use this to reveal more content that may be below or above the current viewport. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_snapshot
function.description: Get a text-based snapshot of the current page's accessibility tree. Returns interactive elements with ref IDs (like @e1, @e2) for browser_click and browser_type. full=false (default): compact view with interactive elements. full=true: complete page content. Snapshots over 8000 chars are truncated or LLM-summarized. Requires browser_navigate first. Note: browser_navigate already returns a compact snapshot — use this to refresh after interactions that change the page, or with full=true for complete content.

full.description: If true, returns complete page content. If false (default), returns compact view with interactive elements only.

function.name: browser_type
function.description: Type text into an input field identified by its ref ID. Clears the field first, then types the new text. Requires browser_navigate and browser_snapshot to be called first.

ref.description: The element reference from the snapshot (e.g., '@e3')

text.description: The text to type into the field

function.name: browser_vision
function.description: Take a screenshot of the current page and analyze it with vision AI. Use this when you need to visually understand what's on the page - especially useful for CAPTCHAs, visual verification challenges, complex layouts, or when the text snapshot doesn't capture important visual information. Returns both the AI analysis and a screenshot_path that you can share with the user by including MEDIA:<screenshot_path> in your response. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

question.description: What you want to know about the page visually. Be specific about what you're looking for.

annotate.description: If true, overlay numbered [N] labels on interactive elements. Each [N] maps to ref @eN for subsequent browser commands. Useful for QA and spatial reasoning about page layout.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_image_search
function.description: Performs an image search using the Brave Search API. Helpful for when you need pictures of people, places, things, graphic design ideas, art inspiration, and more. When relaying results in a markdown environment, it may be helpful to include images in the results (e.g., ![image.title](image.properties.url)).

query.description: The user's search query. Query cannot be empty. Limited to 400 characters and 50 words.

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

count.description: Number of results (1-200, default 50). Combine this parameter with `offset` to paginate search results.

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'strict' - Drops all adult content from search results.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_local_search
function.description:
Brave Local Search API provides enrichments for location search results. Access to this API is available only through the Brave Search API Pro plans; confirm the user's plan before using this tool (if the user does not have a Pro plan, use the brave_web_search tool). Searches for local businesses and places using Brave's Local Search API. Best for queries related to physical locations, businesses, restaurants, services, etc.
    
    Returns detailed information including:
        - Business names and addresses
        - Ratings and review counts
        - Phone numbers and opening hours

    Use this when the query implies 'near me', 'in my area', or mentions specific locations (e.g., 'in San Francisco'). This tool automatically falls back to brave_web_search if no local results are found.

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

ui_lang.description: The language of the UI. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

count.description: Number of results (1-20, default 10). Applies only to web search results (i.e., has no effect on locations, news, videos, etc.)

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'moderate' - Filters explicit content (e.g., images and videos), but allows adult domains in search results. 'strict' - Drops all adult content from search results. The default value is 'moderate'.

freshness.description: Filters search results by when they were discovered. The following values are supported: 'pd' - Discovered within the last 24 hours. 'pw' - Discovered within the last 7 days. 'pm' - Discovered within the last 31 days. 'py' - Discovered within the last 365 days. 'YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD' - Timeframe is also supported by specifying the date range e.g. [REDACTED_PHONE]to[REDACTED_PHONE].

text_decorations.description: Whether display strings (e.g. result snippets) should include decoration markers (e.g. highlighting characters).

spellcheck.description: Whether to spellcheck the provided query.

goggles.description: Goggles act as a custom re-ranking on top of Brave's search index. The parameter supports both a url where the Goggle is hosted or the definition of the Goggle. For more details, refer to the Goggles repository (i.e., https://github.com/brave/goggles-quickstart).

units.description: The measurement units. If not provided, units are derived from search country.

extra_snippets.description: A snippet is an excerpt from a page you get as a result of the query, and extra_snippets allow you to get up to 5 additional, alternative excerpts. Only available under Free AI, Base AI, Pro AI, Base Data, Pro Data and Custom plans.

summary.description: This parameter enables summary key generation in web search results. This is required for summarizer to be enabled.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_news_search
function.description:
This tool searches for news articles using Brave's News Search API based on the user's query. Use it when you need current news information, breaking news updates, or articles about specific topics, events, or entities.
    
    When to use:
        - Finding recent news articles on specific topics
        - Getting breaking news updates
        - Researching current events or trending stories
        - Gathering news sources and headlines for analysis

    Returns a JSON list of news-related results with title, url, and description. Some results may contain snippets of text from the article.
    
    When relaying results in markdown-supporting environments, always cite sources with hyperlinks.
    
    Examples:
        - "According to [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/technology/china-bans/), China bans uncertified and recalled power banks on planes".
        - "The [New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/27/us/technology/ev-sales.html) reports that Tesla's EV sales have increased by 20%".
        - "According to [BBC News](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-65910000), the UK government has announced a new policy to support renewable energy".

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

ui_lang.description: User interface language preferred in response. Usually of the format <language_code>-<country_code>. For more, see RFC 9110.

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'moderate' - Filter out explicit content. 'strict' - Filter out explicit and suggestive content. The default value is 'moderate'.

freshness.description: Filters search results by when they were discovered. The following values are supported: 'pd' - Discovered within the last 24 hours. 'pw' - Discovered within the last 7 Days. 'pm' - Discovered within the last 31 Days. 'py' - Discovered within the last 365 Days. 'YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD' - Timeframe is also supported by specifying the date range e.g. [REDACTED_PHONE]to[REDACTED_PHONE].

extra_snippets.description: A snippet is an excerpt from a page you get as a result of the query, and extra_snippets allow you to get up to 5 additional, alternative excerpts. Only available under Free AI, Base AI, Pro AI, Base Data, Pro Data and Custom plans.

goggles.description: Goggles act as a custom re-ranking on top of Brave's search index. The parameter supports both a url where the Goggle is hosted or the definition of the Goggle. For more details, refer to the Goggles repository (i.e., https://github.com/brave/goggles-quickstart).

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_summarizer
function.description:
Retrieves AI-generated summaries of web search results using Brave's Summarizer API. This tool processes search results to create concise, coherent summaries of information gathered from multiple sources.

    When to use:

    - When you need a concise overview of complex topics from multiple sources
    - For quick fact-checking or getting key points without reading full articles
    - When providing users with summarized information that synthesizes various perspectives
    - For research tasks requiring distilled information from web searches

    Returns a text summary that consolidates information from the search results. Optional features include inline references to source URLs and additional entity information.

    Requirements: Must first perform a web search using brave_web_search with summary=true parameter. Requires a Pro AI subscription to access the summarizer functionality.

key.description: The key is equal to value of field key as part of the Summarizer response model.

entity_info.description: Returns extra entities info with the summary response.

inline_references.description: Adds inline references to the summary response.

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_video_search
function.description:
Searches for videos using Brave's Video Search API and returns structured video results with metadata.

    When to use:
        - When you need to find videos related to a specific topic, keyword, or query.
        - Useful for discovering video content, getting video metadata, or finding videos from specific creators/publishers.

    Returns a JSON list of video-related results with title, url, description, duration, and thumbnail_url.

function.name: mcp_context7_query_docs
function.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

function.name: mcp_context7_read_resource
function.description: Read a resource by URI from MCP server 'context7'

uri.description: URI of the resource to read

function.name: mcp_context7_resolve_library_id
function.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

function.name: mcp_exa_web_fetch_exa
function.description:
Read a webpage's full content as clean markdown. Use after web_search_exa when highlights are insufficient or to read any URL.

Best for: Extracting full content from known URLs. Batch multiple URLs in one call.
Returns: Clean text content and metadata from the page(s).

urls.description: URLs to read. Batch multiple URLs in one call.

maxCharacters.description: Maximum characters to extract per page (must be a positive number, default: 3000)

function.name: mcp_exa_web_search_exa
function.description:
Search the web for any topic and get clean, ready-to-use content.

      Best for: Finding current information, news, facts, people, companies, or answering questions about any topic.
      Returns: Clean text content from top search results.

      Query tips: 
      describe the ideal page, not keywords. "blog post comparing React and Vue performance" not "React vs Vue".
      Use category:people / category:company to search through Linkedin profiles / companies respectively.
      If highlights are insufficient, follow up with web_fetch_exa on the best URLs.

query.description: Natural language search query. Should be a semantically rich description of the ideal page, not just keywords. Optionally include category:<type> (company, people) to focus results — e.g. 'category:people John Doe software engineer'.

numResults.description: Number of search results to return (must be a number, default: 10).

function.name: mcp_filesystem_create_directory
function.description: Create a new directory or ensure a directory exists. Can create multiple nested directories in one operation. If the directory already exists, this operation will succeed silently. Perfect for setting up directory structures for projects or ensuring required paths exist. Only works within allowed directories.

function.name: mcp_filesystem_directory_tree
function.description: Get a recursive tree view of files and directories as a JSON structure. Each entry includes 'name', 'type' (file/directory), and 'children' for directories. Files have no children array, while directories always have a children array (which may be empty). The output is formatted with 2-space indentation for readability. Only works within allowed directories.

function.name: mcp_filesystem_edit_file
function.description: Make line-based edits to a text file. Each edit replaces exact line sequences with new content. Returns a git-style diff showing the changes made. Only works within allowed directories.

dryRun.description: Preview changes using git-style diff format

function.name: [REDACTED_TOKEN]
function.description:
A detailed tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through thoughts.
This tool helps analyze problems through a flexible thinking process that can adapt and evolve.
Each thought can build on, question, or revise previous insights as understanding deepens.

When to use this tool:
- Breaking down complex problems into steps
- Planning and design with room for revision
- Analysis that might need course correction
- Problems where the full scope might not be clear initially
- Problems that require a multi-step solution
- Tasks that need to maintain context over multiple steps
- Situations where irrelevant information needs to be filtered out

Key features:
- You can adjust total_thoughts up or down as you progress
- You can question or revise previous thoughts
- You can add more thoughts even after reaching what seemed like the end
- You can express uncertainty and explore alternative approaches
- Not every thought needs to build linearly - you can branch or backtrack
- Generates a solution hypothesis
- Verifies the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
- Repeats the process until satisfied
- Provides a correct answer

Parameters explained:
- thought: Your current thinking step, which can include:
  * Regular analytical steps
  * Revisions of previous thoughts
  * Questions about previous decisions
  * Realizations about needing more analysis
  * Changes in approach
  * Hypothesis generation
  * Hypothesis verification
- nextThoughtNeeded: True if you need more thinking, even if at what seemed like the end
- thoughtNumber: Current number in sequence (can go beyond initial total if needed)
- totalThoughts: Current estimate of thoughts needed (can be adjusted up/down)
- isRevision: A boolean indicating if this thought revises previous thinking
- revisesThought: If is_revision is true, which thought number is being reconsidered
- branchFromThought: If branching, which thought number is the branching point
- branchId: Identifier for the current branch (if any)
- needsMoreThoughts: If reaching end but realizing more thoughts needed

You should:
1. Start with an initial estimate of needed thoughts, but be ready to adjust
2. Feel free to question or revise previous thoughts
3. Don't hesitate to add more thoughts if needed, even at the "end"
4. Express uncertainty when present
5. Mark thoughts that revise previous thinking or branch into new paths
6. Ignore information that is irrelevant to the current step
7. Generate a solution hypothesis when appropriate
8. Verify the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
9. Repeat the process until satisfied with the solution
10. Provide a single, ideally correct answer as the final output
11. Only set nextThoughtNeeded to false when truly done and a satisfactory answer is reached

nextThoughtNeeded.description: Whether another thought step is needed

totalThoughts.description: Estimated total thoughts needed (numeric value, e.g., 5, 10)

ONLY disable if the function previously had it disabled OR you've confirmed the function body implements custom authentication (e.g., API keys, webhooks) OR the user explicitly requested it be disabled.

files.description: The files to upload. This should include the entrypoint, deno.json, and any relative dependencies. Include the deno.json and deno.jsonc files to configure the Deno runtime (e.g., compiler options, imports) if they exist.

function.name: mcp_supabase_get_publishable_keys
function.description: Gets all publishable API keys for a project, including legacy anon keys (JWT-based) and modern publishable keys (format: sb_publishable_...). Publishable keys are recommended for new applications due to better security and independent rotation. Legacy anon keys are included for compatibility, as many LLMs are pretrained on them. Disabled keys are indicated by the "disabled" field; only use keys where disabled is false or undefined.

function.name: mcp_supabase_search_docs
function.description:
Search the Supabase documentation using GraphQL. Must be a valid GraphQL query.
You should default to calling this even if you think you already know the answer, since the documentation is always being updated.

Below is the GraphQL schema for this tool:

schema{query:RootQueryType}type Guide implements SearchResult{title:String href:String content:String subsections:SubsectionCollection}interface SearchResult{title:String href:String content:String}type SubsectionCollection{edges:[SubsectionEdge!]! nodes:[Subsection!]! totalCount:Int!}type SubsectionEdge{node:Subsection!}type Subsection{title:String href:String content:String}type CLICommandReference implements SearchResult{title:String href:String content:String}type ManagementApiReference implements SearchResult{title:String href:String content:String}type ClientLibraryFunctionReference implements SearchResult{title:String href:String content:String language:Language! methodName:String}enum Language{JAVASCRIPT SWIFT DART CSHARP KOTLIN PYTHON}type TroubleshootingGuide implements SearchResult{title:String href:String content:String}type RootQueryType{schema:String! searchDocs(query:String!,limit:Int):SearchResultCollection error(code:String!,service:Service!):Error errors(first:Int after:String last:Int before:String service:Service code:String):ErrorCollection}type SearchResultCollection{edges:[SearchResultEdge!]! nodes:[SearchResult!]! totalCount:Int!}type SearchResultEdge{node:SearchResult!}type Error{code:String! service:Service! httpStatusCode:Int message:String}enum Service{AUTH REALTIME STORAGE}type ErrorCollection{edges:[ErrorEdge!]! nodes:[Error!]! pageInfo:PageInfo! totalCount:Int!}type ErrorEdge{node:Error! cursor:String!}type PageInfo{hasNextPage:Boolean! hasPreviousPage:Boolean! startCursor:String endCursor:String}

function.name: web_extract
function.description: Extract content from web page URLs. Returns page content in markdown format. Also works with PDF URLs (arxiv papers, documents, etc.) — pass the PDF link directly and it converts to markdown text. Pages under 5000 chars return full markdown; larger pages are LLM-summarized and capped at ~5000 chars per page. Pages over 2M chars are refused. If a URL fails or times out, use the browser tool to access it instead.

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from (max 5 URLs per call)

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web for information. Returns up to 5 results by default with titles, URLs, and descriptions. The query is passed through to the configured backend, so operators such as site:domain, filetype:pdf, intitle:word, -term, and "exact phrase" may work when the backend supports them.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web. You may include backend-supported operators such as site:example.com, filetype:pdf, intitle:word, -term, or "exact phrase".

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 5.

        "title": "Residence of Foreign Nationals in Paraguay",
        "description": "Consulado General de la República del Paraguay en Los Angeles-CA, Estados Unidos de América :: Residence of Foreign Nationals in Paraguay 

Choose your language: Spanish/ English

---

#### Residence of Foreign Nationals in Paraguay

 

All travelers, Foreign and Paraguayan Nationals must register their entering and exiting in Paraguay and need to pass through control post, checkpoints open by the corresponding authorities.

The admission, entry, permanence and exit of foreigners into Paraguayan territory, is strictly controlled and recorded under the administration of -“Direccion General of Migraciones” – DGMA- this office is in charge of the execution of national migratory policies. Its principal function is to ensure protection and to provide security of the persons travelling through checkpoints, contributing with the prevention of transnational crimes such as Human Traffic and others.

The Immigration Office- DGM - has the authority of supervise legal status of the foreigners in the country and in case of either foreign nationals or employers (that eventually provide work and jobs to the immigrants), commit a legal offence, breaking the Paraguayan Immigration Law, they will be sanctioned by the Immigration Office –DGM-

The foreign nationals admitted in the country, are eligible for residence and can come in person to the Immigration Office “Direccion General de Migraciones”, where will formally able to apply for Permanent or Temporary Residence.

In order to present the application for the Residence, some documents will be needed and must be enclosed to the forms. Please, read the requirements and procedures to carry out in order to obtain the Residence. Check following files:

More information: website : www.migraciones.gov.py( Spanish only)

---

→ DOWNLOAD TEMPORARY RESIDENCE REQUIREMENTS

---

→ DOWNLOAD PERMANENT RESIDENCE REQUIREMENTS

---",

Let our team of knowledgeable expats and experienced local legal professionals guide you through the process of obtaining Paraguayan Residency

        "url": "https://n30paraguay.com/en/permanent-residency/",
        "title": "Permanent Residency Advisory - N30 Paraguay",
        "description": "Permanent Residency Advisory - N30 Paraguay
[...]
# Immediate Permanent Residence in Paraguay by Investment (SUACE)
[...]
For entrepreneurs and investors who want a solid residence from the start, linked to a real investment in Paraguay, with legal and logistical support and an international tax perspective (N30 Global + N30 Paraguay).
[...]
- Via SUACE with a minimum required investment of USD 70,000 (or equivalent).
- Full coordination: documentation, notary, agenda, offices, and follow-up.
- No improvisation: checklist + prior review to avoid unnecessary trips.
- Service in Spanish and English (WhatsApp/Email).
- Serious EU tax focus: if your goal is Spain/Europe, we handle it methodically.
[...]
## SUACE: the route to permanent residence from the start (with investment)
[...]
The SUACE route is designed for investors/entrepreneurs who want to obtain permanent residence from the outset, linked to an investment project and formal documentation. It requires, among other elements, a minimum investment and appropriate documentation/planning.
[...]
- you want permanent residence instead of waiting for longer transition processes,
- you are willing to invest and properly document the project,
- you want to minimize uncertainty and do it with professional coordination.
[...]
## Who is this service for (and who it is not)?
[...]
- It fits if…
- You have capital available for a real investment (reference minimum USD 70,000).
- You want immediate permanent residence as a base for life/an international plan.
- You are looking for a guided, professional process, with partners and properly prepared documentation.
- You care about doing it without surprises (banks, paperwork, requirements, timelines).
[...]
## What our Investor Pack (SUACE) includes
[...]
- Includes:
- Fit-assessment call + personalized checklist (to confirm if SUACE applies).
- Planning and timeline of the SUACE process (step by step).
- Prior document review before traveling (to avoid mistakes).
- Coordination of power of attorney / notary / certified copies as needed.
- Support and coordination with local partners (immigration lawyer, management firm, etc.).
- Management of permanent residence within the SUACE route (with follow-up).
- Paraguayan ID card within the corresponding workflow.
- Local transfers to required offices during the process (according to agenda).
- Remote support via WhatsApp/Email throughout the entire process.
- Operational closure: recommended next steps after approval.
[...]
## How it works
[...]
We confirm whether SUACE is the right route or if another option suits you better.
[...]
We guide you to prepare documents, translations, and required legalizations.
[...]
We coordinate the alignment of the project with the program requirements (with partners).
[...]
### Step 4 — Travel and procedures in Asunción
[...]
Closed agenda, accompaniment, and transfers to offices.
[...]
Follow-up until the result and “next steps” according to your objective.
[...]
What is the minimum investment?
[...]
The official program documentation mentions USD 70,000 (or equivalent). On the call, we explain how it is structured in your case.
[...]
Is permanent residence the same as tax residence?
[...]
No. Legal residence and taxation are different things. If your goal is Spain/EU, we address it with methodology (N30 Global).
[...]
Normally there are in-person procedures. We will tell you the minimum required and what can be done by power of attorney/representation.
[...]
## Do you want immediate permanent residence with investment?
[...]
Book a free fit-assessment call (15 min) and we will tell you:
[...]
- if SUACE is the right route,
- exactly what you need,
- and the step-by-step plan with timelines and costs.",

        "title": "Paraguay Residency | Ultimate 2026 Guide to Residency | Paraguay Sovereign",
        "description": "Paraguay residency is a legal immigration status that grants foreign nationals a residence permit to live, work, and conduct business in the Republic of Paraguay. But for most international applicants, the appeal extends far beyond the right to reside in this South American nation. Paraguay residency provides access to one of the most favorable tax regimes in the Western Hemisphere-a territorial tax system that can allow 0% on qualifying foreign-sourced income; treatment depends on source characterization, operational facts, and compliance.
[...]
Unlike traditional immigration programs that require you to physically relocate, Paraguay's residency framework is uniquely suited to the modern, location-independent lifestyle. Maintenance depends on stage: temporary residency has annual presence compliance expectations, while permanent residency allows up to a 3-year absence window. This makes Paraguay an exceptional choice for digital nomads, remote workers, online entrepreneurs, and anyone seeking a legitimate tax optimization strategy without uprooting their life.
[...]
Paraguay offers two primary pathways to legal residency: Paraguay temporary residency and Paraguay permanent residency. Each suits different circumstances and goals:
[...]
The standard pathway chosen by approximately 90% of foreign applicants. Temporary residency is valid for one year and is renewable indefinitely. After maintaining temporary residency for 21-24 months, you become eligible to upgrade to permanent residency status.
[...]
Both pathways ultimately lead to the same destination: legal residency in Paraguay with access to the territorial tax system. The choice between them depends on your timeline, investment capacity, and whether you plan to actively conduct business in Paraguay.
[...]
designers, writers

providing services to international clients

% on that foreign

Paraguay offers a low-cost, low-maintenance "Plan B" that provides optionality without requiring immediate relocation. Whether your concern is political instability, currency risk, or simply the desire for a backup option, Paraguay residency sits quietly in your portfolio until if and when you need it.
[...]
Perhaps the most striking feature of Paraguay residency is what it doesn't require: your physical presence. Unlike countries that mandate 183+ days per year to maintain tax residency, Paraguay has no minimum stay requirement. Temporary residency requires visiting at least once every 12 months. Permanent residency requires visiting at least once every 36 months.
[...]
Unlike programs that require extensive documentation, interviews, or waiting periods measured in years, Paraguay residency can typically be completed in 60-120 days. The documentation requirements are reasonable, the government

are accessible, and the process is well-established with predictable outcomes. Our approval

The fundamental principle: if income is generated within Paraguay's territory, it's taxable. If income originates from outside Paraguay, it's not taxable-regardless of whether you're a resident.

With the Constancia and all required documents, submission to the Dirección Nacional de Migraciones. Physical presence of the applicant in Asunción is generally required to sign declarations before the official. A stay of a few days is sufficient.

70,000 USD committed over 10 years in a Paraguayan company incorporated through the SUACE (Law No. 6984/22). The investment must be real, traceable through banking records, and declared in the Constancia de Inversionista Extranjero. It does not need to be paid in full on day one.

        "description": "New Digital Residency Platform Set To Launch In Paraguay

Search

Paraguay is moving forward with plans to modernise its residency system for newcomers. The Ministry of Information and Communication Technologies (MITIC) and the National Directorate of Migration (DNM) are developing a new digital residency platform designed to streamline and simplify residency procedures.

The initiative centres on the creation of the Comprehensive Residents System, a tool aimed at reducing paperwork and improving efficiency through digitalisation. Authorities say the project will help speed up processing times while also strengthening transparency across institutions.

## Digital residency platform for migration services

As part of the development process, a technical team from MITIC’s Vice Ministry of Technologies recently met with representatives from Migration to review the first proposals for the system. The meeting focused on defining operational guidelines and mapping out the implementation phase of the digital platform.

The core objective is to redesign existing residency procedures. This includes eliminating repetitive administrative steps and transforming traditional paper-based processes into streamlined digital workflows. By doing so, officials expect to reduce the need for in-person appointments and accelerate the handling of applications.

The platform will also incorporate automated validation and monitoring tools. These features will allow officials to manage applications electronically, ensuring full traceability through auditable digital records. Each action taken within a case file will be recorded, contributing to greater institutional accountability.

## Aligning with Paraguay’s digital transformation agenda

The project forms part of Paraguay’s broader push towards digital government. In recent years, MITIC has led efforts to expand online public services and promote the use of digital signatures and electronic documentation across state institutions.

By transitioning to a paperless management model for residency procedures, the new system reflects that wider strategy. Authorities believe the digital approach will not only improve internal efficiency but also enhance user experience for applicants.

Another key element of the platform is decentralisation. Once implemented, newcomers applying for residency will be able to complete procedures from anywhere with internet access. This could significantly benefit applicants living outside the capital or those unable to attend offices during standard working hours.

Through this joint initiative, the institutions aim to modernise one of the country’s most important administrative processes, reinforcing Paraguay’s commitment to accessible and technology-driven public services.

We don't just sell advice. We deliver the physical tools of a global citizen. In 60-120 days, you will hold a legal Paraguayan ID (Cédula) and Tax ID (RUC), giving you access to one of the world's last territorial tax systems.

### Territorial Tax System

Paraguay's territorial tax system may exempt foreign-sourced income from taxation. Treatment depends on source characterization, where economic activity occurs, and proper compliance. See tax explainer.

### Minimal Physical Presence

Temporary stage: visit annually. Permanent stage: visit every 3 years. It is the ultimate "Plan B" that doesn't tie you down.

### Path to Citizenship

A strategic gateway to a Mercosur passport. Eligible for naturalization after three years of permanent residency. Visa-free access to 140+ countries.

#### Program Changes Expected in 2026

Common Questions

## You Have Questions.

#### Do I need to live in Paraguay to maintain residency?

No. Paraguay has no minimum stay requirement. Temporary residency requires visiting at least once every 12 months. Permanent residency requires visiting at least once every 36 months. Most of our clients continue living and working wherever they choose.

#### Is the territorial tax system legitimate?

Yes, Paraguay's territorial tax system is 100% legal and recognized internationally. However, what counts as "foreign-sourced" depends on where the work is performed and your business structure. Income earned while physically in Paraguay (even for foreign clients) is typically taxable. See complete tax guide.

#### Do I need a medical exam or bank deposit?

No to both. The medical exam requirement was removed, and the $5,000 bank deposit was eliminated in 2022. These are outdated requirements you may see on other websites. Paraguay now has one of the most accessible residency programs in Latin America.

#### How long does the residency process take?

The complete process typically takes 60-120 days from document preparation to receiving your Cédula (national ID). Timeline depends on document readiness, Migraciones processing times, and your availability for the in-country visit. Most clients complete everything within 3 months.

#### What documents do I need for Paraguay residency?

Requirements include: valid passport, birth certificate (apostilled), police clearance (apostilled), proof of income (bank statements or employment letter), and marriage certificate if applicable. All foreign documents must be translated into Spanish. We provide a complete personalized checklist during your consultation.

All protocols start with a free structural review. No upfront commitment. No pressure.

### Compliance Pre-Check

Your eligibility and documents are verified remotely before any travel or on-site commitment is required.

### Statutory Fees Included

All Paraguayan filing, notary, and administrative fees are included in the project budget.

### Administrative Representation

Full preparation and notary costs for the local Power of Attorney (POA) are covered.

### On-Ground Coordination

Personal accompaniment in Asunción for all mandatory government visits and biometrics.

### Multilingual Support

100% communication in English. No Spanish required at any stage of the process.

### Secure Document Handling

Encrypted digital storage and a dedicated support channel throughout the execution.

Trust & Transparency

No upfront payments. Readiness is confirmed remotely, and payment is made on-site in Asunción upon signing the Power of Attorney.

## Transparent pricing. Third-party costs explained

To keep our project fees fixed and fair, variable third-party costs are not bundled. These are paid directly or at cost, with full transparency.

### Official Translations

Documents must be translated into Spanish by a certified translator to be legally valid in Paraguay.

Execution Cost

Varies by document type

### Home-Country Legalization

Costs for issuing Birth Certificates, Police Records, and Apostilles are paid directly to your local authorities.

Execution Cost

Varies by local jurisdiction

### International Shipping

Optional secure door-to-door courier delivery of your documents via DHL if not collected in person.

Execution Cost

Standard DHL rates apply

## Real scenarios. Defensible outcomes

We protect client privacy and avoid fake testimonials. Here are the typical execution profiles we handle and why they chose our protocols.

THE “VALUE” INVESTOR (EU)

The Goal Wanted a defensible tax residency setup (RUC + ROK) while avoiding the $5k+ fees charged by large agencies.

The Execution We executed the Staged Protocol, spreading the process across two visits to optimize overall cost.

The Result Full compliance achieved for $2,490, with a verifiable audit trail and no unnecessary overhead.

THE EFFICIENCY SEEKER (Canada)

The Goal Required a Plan B residency solution but could not justify multiple long-haul trips or open-ended timelines.

The Execution We applied the Efficiency Route, completing all mandatory filings during a single 3-day visit.

The Result Residency and ID applications submitted without a return trip, minimizing both time commitment and travel costs.

THE “STRUCTURE FIRST” NOMAD (Digital)

The Goal Already location-independent, but faced account freezes due to the absence of a recognized tax residency.

The Execution We established a verified address, tax certificate (ROK), and residency within one structured process.

The Result Banking compliance restored and a legal home base secured, without forcing a lifestyle change.

★ See all verified reviews on Trustpilot →

Do I need to pay the full budget upfront?

No. We first verify your documents and eligibility at no cost. Payment is made only in Asunción, when you sign the Power of Attorney and the process is officially initiated. Until then, there is no financial commitment or sunk cost.

Are government and notary fees really included?

Yes. Our project budgets are all-inclusive. This covers all official government fees, notary services, filings, and local administrative costs in Paraguay. The only external costs are your personal documents from your home country and their certified translations — nothing else appears later.

Why are your protocols priced lower than big agencies?

Because we don’t sell relocation theater. We focus on smart execution — only the steps that are legally required and time-efficient. No luxury offices. No sales layers. No unnecessary intermediaries. Lower overhead means you pay for results, not for the agency’s lifestyle.

What happens if my application is rejected?

We eliminate this risk upfront. We only accept clients after a successful document pre-check. If your case isn't defensible or your documents aren't compliant, we will tell you immediately and won't proceed. To date, every client who has passed our pre-check has successfully secured their status.

      "content": "Residency for Entrepreneurs - N30 Paraguay

# Residency in Paraguay for European entrepreneurs and nomads

We help you obtain your temporary residence and Paraguayan ID with step-by-step explanations, in-office accompaniment, and full coordination. And if your goal is tax optimization, the Platinum Pack includes a defensible plan with an international perspective.

- EAS/SA company incorporation according to your case (no “one-size-fits-all”).
- RUC and tax registration of the company (essential step to operate).
- Banking assistance (no approval promises: we prepare you and accompany you).
- Serious approach: taxes and obligations explained in plain human language (no smoke).

Book a free call

Message us on WhatsApp

## Why N30 Paraguay?

Most providers in Paraguay process paperwork. We do something different: we connect your tax reality—whether you’re in Spain, Europe, or anywhere else—with local execution in Paraguay.

### Global vision

We understand the real fears of European entrepreneurs (Tax Authority, center of interests, typical mistakes).

### Turnkey local execution

Verified partners (managers/lawyers/notary) + accompaniment and logistics.

### No magic promises

Clarity on requirements, limits, and scenarios. No smoke.

## Choose the package that fits you best

Want only the residence? → Silver or GoldWant to use Paraguay as a strategic exit from Spain/Europe? → Platinum

### SILVER PACK — Temporary Residence (Standard process / 2 trips)

2.500 €

Ideal if: you want a complete service and don’t mind following the standard process (usually 2 short trips or 1 long trip).

Includes (turnkey):

- Temporary Residence (full management).
- Paraguayan ID (within the process flow).
- RUC (Paraguay tax number) (note: it does not by itself equal tax residency).
- Personalized checklist of documents and steps to follow.
- Pre-review of documentation before traveling (to avoid surprises).
- Certified sworn translations included (up to [X] documents / [X] pages; extras quoted separately).
- Notarial power of attorney and related document preparation.
- Notarized copies of required documents.
- Coordinated agenda of all necessary appointments.
- Airport transfer (depending on arrival schedule/plan).
- Transportation to all required offices during the process.
- In-person accompaniment at offices in Spanish/English.
- Remote support via WhatsApp/Email throughout the process.
- Delivery of final documentation to the address you indicate if needed (DHL not included).

Book a free call

### GOLD PACK — Optimized Temporary Residence (1 trip)

2.900 €

Ideal if: you’re coming from Spain/Europe and want to minimize trips and friction with an optimized agenda.

Includes everything in the Silver Pack, plus:

- “Optimized trip” planning to complete most of the process in 1 trip.
- Priority coordination (proactive follow-up and faster response times).
- Enhanced document review (more control before traveling to Paraguay).
- “5 days in Asunción” guide (logistics recommendations for the process).
- “First steps in Paraguay” checklist (operational: banks/basic procedures, not deep tax).
- Extended post-process support for [X] weeks for operational questions.

I want to do it in 1 trip

### PLATINUM PACK — Residence + EU → Paraguay Tax Plan (Recommended)

4.200€

4.400€ (without pack)

Ideal if: your real objective is to exit Spain/EU tax control with a serious, defensible plan—not just “having a paper.”

Includes everything in the Gold Pack, plus:

- Strategic session with N30 (international taxation) to understand your case (income, structure, countries, risks).• Written EU → Paraguay plan (personalized) with:• current scenario vs. target scenario,• disengagement checklist (housing/company/center of interests, etc.),• typical risk points and how to mitigate them,• prioritized recommendations.• 90-day roadmap (execution plan after the process).• Final post-residence Q&A to ensure you understand how to operate without making common mistakes.• Priority support for [X] weeks for critical questions.

I want a defensible plan

## Full transparency: what’s included and what’s not

- Included in all packages:
- Full coordination of the process and appointments.
- In-person accompaniment + remote support.
- Local logistics (transfers to offices).
- Document management + associated notary.
- Service in Spanish/English.

- Not included:
- Flights, accommodation, and meals.
- Apostilles and certificates issued in your country of origin (if applicable).
- International shipments (DHL/courier).
- Guaranteed bank account opening (we can assist, but the bank decides).
- Company incorporation, ongoing accounting, holding structures (separate services).
- Tax residency / deep Spain/EU tax planning (included only in Platinum).
- Any service outside the agreed scope or extraordinary regulatory changes (you’ll be informed before incurring additional costs).

## How it works (step by step)

### 01 — Preparation (before the trip)

We give you a clear checklist and review your documentation to avoid mistakes and wasted trips.

### 02 — Trip to Asunción

We receive you and accompany you with a coordinated agenda. Transfers to the required offices included.

### 03 — Residence processing

Application submission and process follow-up with our partners.

### 04 — ID and closure

When applicable, we finalize with the ID and give you the “operational closure” (what to do next). If you’re in Platinum, this also includes the tax plan and roadmap.

Note: timelines depend on documentation and administrative processing times. On the call, we give you a realistic estimate.

## Real cases

### Case 1 — Spanish entrepreneur (digital services)

Goal: residence + orderly exit from Spain.Solution: Platinum Pack → EU→Paraguay plan + 90-day roadmap.

### Case 2 — European nomad (optimized trip)

Goal: resolve everything in a single trip.Solution: Gold Pack → optimized agenda + full accompaniment.

### Case 3 — Residence managed with local partners

Goal: standard process with maximum clarity.Solution: Silver Pack → complete process with remote support.

## Frequently asked questions (FAQs)

Legal residence = tax residence?

Not necessarily. Legal residence is the permission to reside. Tax residence depends on facts and ties (presence, center of interests, etc.). If your goal is tax-related (Spain/EU), the Platinum Pack includes the plan and personalized checklist.

Does the RUC mean tax residency?

No. The RUC is Paraguay’s tax number. It can be part of your setup, but it does not by itself equal “being a tax resident.”

Can I do it in a single trip?

Yes, with the Gold Pack (optimized trip), as long as your documentation is ready and the administrative calendar allows it.

How long does the whole process take?

It depends on the case and the administration. On the call, we give indicative timelines based on the pack and your documentation.

Do I need to live in Paraguay all year?

It depends on your objective (legal vs. tax residence) and your global picture. That’s why we recommend Platinum if your concern is the Tax Authority/EU.

Do you help with a bank account?

We can assist/guide you, but approval depends on the bank. We don’t promise guaranteed openings.

What if I’m missing a document or something unexpected comes up?

That’s why we do a prior review and checklist. If something extraordinary arises, we communicate it transparently and propose the most efficient solution.

## Not sure which package to choose?

Book a free fit call (15 min) and we’ll tell you:

- whether Paraguay fits your case,
- which pack makes the most sense,
- and what the next steps are.

Book a free call

## Practical guide: residence process step by step (Asunción in 5 days)

        "description": "New Digital Residency Platform Set To Launch In Paraguay

Search

See all results

Paraguay is moving forward with plans to modernise its residency system for newcomers. The Ministry of Information and Communication Technologies (MITIC) and the National Directorate of Migration (DNM) are developing a new digital residency platform designed to streamline and simplify residency procedures.

The initiative centres on the creation of the Comprehensive Residents System, a tool aimed at reducing paperwork and improving efficiency through digitalisation. Authorities say the project will help speed up processing times while also strengthening transparency across institutions.

## Digital residency platform for migration services

As part of the development process, a technical team from MITIC’s Vice Ministry of Technologies recently met with representatives from Migration to review the first proposals for the system. The meeting focused on defining operational guidelines and mapping out the implementation phase of the digital platform.

The core objective is to redesign existing residency procedures. This includes eliminating repetitive administrative steps and transforming traditional paper-based processes into streamlined digital workflows. By doing so, officials expect to reduce the need for in-person appointments and accelerate the handling of applications.

The platform will also incorporate automated validation and monitoring tools. These features will allow officials to manage applications electronically, ensuring full traceability through auditable digital records. Each action taken within a case file will be recorded, contributing to greater institutional accountability.

## Aligning with Paraguay’s digital transformation agenda

The project forms part of Paraguay’s broader push towards digital government. In recent years, MITIC has led efforts to expand online public services and promote the use of digital signatures and electronic documentation across state institutions.

By transitioning to a paperless management model for residency procedures, the new system reflects that wider strategy. Authorities believe the digital approach will not only improve internal efficiency but also enhance user experience for applicants.

Another key element of the platform is decentralisation. Once implemented, newcomers applying for residency will be able to complete procedures from anywhere with internet access. This could significantly benefit applicants living outside the capital or those unable to attend offices during standard working hours.

Through this joint initiative, the institutions aim to modernise one of the country’s most important administrative processes, reinforcing Paraguay’s commitment to accessible and technology-driven public services.

[...]
South America's hidden gem — 0% tax on foreign income, one of the world's easiest permanent residency programs, rock-bottom living costs, and a warm subtropical climate year-round
[...]
Paraguay is the continent's best-kept secret for cost-conscious expats and tax-savvy digital nomads. A landlocked nation of 7.4 million between Argentina, Brazil, and Bolivia, it offers what few countries can match: immediate permanent residency through the SUACE investor program (from $70,000 over 10 years), a strict territorial tax system that levies 0% on all foreign-sourced income, and a cost of living where $1,000–$1,500/month buys a genuinely comfortable life in the capital. Asuncion — a subtropical city of 2.5 million metro residents — has quietly developed modern coworking spaces, reliable 50–100 Mbps fiber internet in central neighborhoods, and a growing international dining scene. Ciudad del Este, on the Brazilian border, is South America's second-largest free-trade zone. Paraguay is not polished — infrastructure outside Asuncion can be rough, bureaucracy is slow, and English is rare — but for expats who value financial freedom, low regulation, and authentic Latin American culture without the tourist-trap prices, it delivers extraordinary value.
[...]
The SUACE investor program grants immediate permanent residency — not temporary, permanent — with a commitment to invest $70,000 over 10 years ($7,000/year). Processing can take as little as 45 days. There is no minimum stay requirement to maintain residency, and citizenship is available after just 3 years. Few countries offer a faster or cheaper path to a second passport.
[...]
🛂Paraguay offers one of the world's fastest and most accessible permanent residency programs. The SUACE investor visa grants immediate permanent residency for a $70,000 investment commitment over 10 years. Temporary residency is available for employees and retirees.         "title": "Paraguay Residency in 90 Days: Legal Step-by-Step Guide (2026)",
        "description": "While Europe tightens its Golden Visa programs, the US freezes its EB-5, and Caribbean passport programs raise prices year after year, Paraguay has quietly maintained one of the lowest investment thresholds in the world for residency by investment: USD 70,000. With that, you can obtain direct permanent residency — skipping the two-year temporary residency that the standard route requires — and access citizenship in as little as 4 years.
[...]
## The Residency by Investment Route: How the SUACE Program Works
[...]
The official program is SUACE (Sistema Único de Acreditación de Capital Extranjero), administered by Paraguay's Ministry of Industry and Commerce. It allows direct permanent residency, bypassing the two years of temporary residency.
[...]
Option 1: Minimum investment of USD 70,000. Capital must go into productive activity in Paraguay. Not a donation or fee. Can be deployed over up to 10 years via a structured business plan.
[...]
Option 2: Create at least 5 Paraguayan jobs. At ~USD 400/month minimum wage, five employees ≈ USD 2,000/month — less than one full-time employee in the US, UK, or Western Europe.
[...]
## The Complete Roadmap: From Investment to Passport in 4–5 Years
[...]
Stage 1 — Document Preparation (~1 month): Birth certificate, criminal background checks, medical certificate, etc., translated and apostilled. Stage 2 — Temporary Residency (2–4 months): For SUACE, this step can be bypassed in favor of direct permanent residency. Paraguay Temporary vs. Permanent Residency. Stage 3 — Cédula (45 days): National ID; required for banks, contracts, official business. How to Obtain Life and Residency in Paraguay. Stage 4 — Permanent Residency (3–4 months): Once approved, the 3-year clock toward citizenship starts. Stage 5 — Naturalization (from 3 years of permanent residency): Basic Spanish (A1) required. Courts typically 6–12 months. Realistic total: 4 to 5 years.
[...]

        "description": "Paraguay Investor Permanent Residency (SUACE)
[...]
Paraguay operates one of the world's most accessible and cost-efficient pathways to permanent residency through its Investor Permanent Residency program, administered by SUACE (Subsecretaría de Estado de Administración de Centros de Atención al Extranjero). The program qualifies applicants through business investment rather than passive capital deposit or real estate purchase. You establish a company in Paraguay with a nominal minimum capital of US$70,000. This capital requirement is deployed over a ten-year period, not as an upfront lump sum. The investment structure creates genuine commercial presence while spreading the financial commitment across a decade.
[...]
The application follows a two-phase sequence. Upon initial approval from Paraguayan immigration authorities, you travel to Paraguay to formally establish your company and submit your permanent residency application in person. Permanent residency is typically approved within 45 days of completing company formation and filing. There are no physical presence requirements to obtain or maintain permanent residency status, you are not obligated to reside in Paraguay to keep your residency active. This separation of legal residency from physical presence makes the program operationally flexible for internationally mobile entrepreneurs.
[...]
After three years of holding permanent residency, you become eligible to apply for Paraguayan naturalization. Citizenship eligibility introduces a presence requirement that does not apply to residency itself: you must have spent at least six months annually in Paraguay during the three years preceding your citizenship application. Successful naturalization grants you a Paraguayan passport providing visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to 141 countries worldwide. The program's structure, low capital threshold, extended deployment timeline, rapid residency approval, and clear three-year pathway to citizenship, positions it as one of the most straightforward residence-by-investment frameworks globally.
[...]
Company formation drives qualification. You must establish a Paraguayan company with declared minimum capital of US$70,000. This capital deploys over a ten-year period, not as an upfront deposit. Full investment is not mandatory if you advance to citizenship before the ten years expire.
[...]
Travel to Paraguay is mandatory. You cannot complete the process remotely. Once preliminary approval is granted, you travel to Paraguay to establish your company through an appointed representative at the SUACE office and submit your permanent residency application in person.
[...]
No physical presence requirement applies to permanent residency acquisition or maintenance. You may live elsewhere after approval. Your residency status remains valid without mandatory annual stays in Paraguay.
[...]
Citizenship eligibility after three years introduces a presence obligation that does not apply to residency. To qualify for naturalization, you must have spent at least six months per year in Paraguay during the three years immediately preceding your citizenship application. Unlike agencies or individual consultants, our team has legal expertise, professional liability, and in-depth knowledge of local administration. Each file is managed rigorously and on a case-by-case basis.",

{"path": "/root/.hermes/hermes-agent/competitor_research_report.md", "content": "# Competitor Research Report: Paraguay Relocation/Residency Market

**Date:** May 7, 2026
**Context:** Competitors to Nexa Paraguay (nexa.paragu-ai.com) — relocation services for Europeans moving to Paraguay.

**Nexa Paraguay's known offerings:**
- Base ($2,900 residency)
- Business ($4,400+ residency+company+bank)
- Investor ($6,900+ includes 12mo support)
- Targets Dutch/Belgian/German/English Europeans

---

## 1. WeParaguay (weparaguay.com)

**Website:** https://weparaguay.com

**Services:**
- Temporary residency ($1,400 Basic - $4,500 VIP)
- SUACE Investor Pass ($5,500) — direct permanent via $150-200k investment
- SUACE Business ($6,500) — direct permanent via $70k business investment
- US LLC formation ($1,500 add-on)
- Company formation in Paraguay ($1,990 setup + $90-220/mo accounting)
- Local concierge/integration ($500-$3,500)
- Tax/fiscal planning ($300-$1,000)
- Real estate investment & property management ($1,500 fee + 20% mgmt)
- PR upgrade ($1,400-$2,400)

**Pricing breakdown:**
- Basic: $1,400 (~6mo, 3 trips, residency + cedula)
- Silver: $2,200 (~90-120 days, 2 trips, +RUC + bank account)
- Gold: $2,850 (~60-90 days, 1 trip, +driver's license)
- VIP: $4,500 (~45-60 days, 1 trip, +welcome dinner, global shipping, 12mo support)
- SUACE Investor: $5,500 (~45 days, 1 trip, permanent residency)
- SUACE Business: $6,500 (~45 days, 1 trip, incl company formation)

**Unique Selling Points:**
- Co-founder Osher personally went through the process
- Direct access to Osher (not call centers)
- US + Paraguay combined setup (LLC + residency)
- Strong community aspect (Zoom sessions, in-person meetups)
- Crypto-friendly payments
- Tax/fiscal planning included in higher tiers
- 6-12 months ongoing support depending on package
- Service Completion Commitment (formal T&C)

**AI/Automation:** No visible AI/automation. Human-led service via WhatsApp/Email. Website is standard Webflow-type.

**Target Audience:** Global, but content clearly targets English-speaking entrepreneurs, digital nomads, retirees, investors.

---

## 2. Destination Paraguay (destination-paraguay.com)
*(Note: Could not find a specific "Bureau Paraguay" — this is the closest French-oriented competitor)*

**Website:** https://destination-paraguay.com/en/

**Services:**
- Temporary residence (2 trips: ~EUR 2,500)
- Tax residence (1 trip, 7 days: EUR 2,900)
- Permanent residence (EUR 3,500)
- Accounting services
- Bank account opening
- Domiciliation & invoices

**Pricing:**
- Basic Temporary: EUR 2,500 (2 trips, no tax residency)
- Tax Residence: EUR 2,900 (1 trip, 7 days, includes RUC + tax cert + bank account + SIM)
- Permanent: EUR 3,500 (includes 1 year free accounting)

**Unique Selling Points:**
- French-speaking team — targets French-speaking expats primarily
- Law firm based in Asuncion
- 60-day processing claimed
- Bonuses: bank account, SIM card, "address book"
- Turnkey approach — pick up from airport

**AI/Automation:** None visible. Traditional law firm model.

---

## 3. Paraguay Sovereign (paraguaysovereign.com)

**Website:** https://paraguaysovereign.com

**Services:**
- Essential: $2,290 (residency + cedula, translations, document review)
- Premium: $3,190 (adds RUC, 12mo RUC filing, banking intro)
- Investor: $5,490 (SUACE route, company formation, tax strategy)
- Bitcoin/crypto payment accepted (5% discount)

**Pricing:**
- Essential: $2,290 ($2,176 crypto)
- Premium: $3,190 ($3,030 crypto)
- Investor: $5,490 ($5,215 crypto)

**Unique Selling Points:**
- 60-120 day timeline
- Strong comparison table (Paraguay vs Panama vs Costa Rica vs Portugal)
- Educational content-driven approach
- Bitcoin discount (5%)
- 98% approval rate claim
- 50% deposit, 50% before trip
- Payment plans available (2-3 installments)

**AI/Automation:** No visible AI automation. Standard website with strategy call booking.

---

## 4. Paraguay Simply (paraguaysimply.com)

**Website:** https://paraguaysimply.com

**Services/Pricing:**
*Residency Routes (no tax):*
- Settlement Route: $1,480 (6-7mo, 1 long or 3 short trips)
- Efficiency Route: $1,890 (4mo, 1 short 3-day trip) — MOST POPULAR
- Priority Route: $2,180 (3mo, 1 short 3-day trip)

*Tax Compliance Protocols:*
- Staged Protocol: $2,490 (3-4mo, 2 trips, ROK + RUC + 3mo accounting)
- One-Trip Protocol: $2,990 (3-4mo, 1 trip 5 days, ROK + 1yr lease + 12mo accounting)

**Unique Selling Points:**
- European coordination + on-ground in Paraguay
- "No upfront payment" — pay in Asuncion when signing POA
- Fixed budgets, all government fees included
- Positioned as anti-luxury ("we don't sell relocation theater")
- Focus on defensible tax residency (ROK certificate)
- Compliance pre-check before accepting clients
- Trustpilot reviews

**AI/Automation:** Automated eligibility checker on website. Process appears standard human-led otherwise.

---

## 5. N30 Paraguay (n30paraguay.com)

**Website:** https://n30paraguay.com/en/residency-in-paraguay/

**Services:**
- Silver Pack (Standard, 2 trips): EUR 2,500
- Gold Pack (Optimized, 1 trip): EUR 2,900
- Platinum Pack (Residence + EU tax exit plan): EUR 4,200-4,400

**Unique Selling Points:**
- Specifically targets European entrepreneurs (especially Spanish)
- Strong tax exit planning for Spain/EU clients
- Written EU-to-Paraguay tax plan in Platinum
- 90-day roadmap for tax disengagement
- Strategic session with N30 (international taxation perspective)

**AI/Automation:** None. Human service with WhatsApp/email support.

---

## 6. Terravida (terravida.polsia.app) — AI Competitor

**Website:** http://terravida.polsia.app/

**Services:**
- AI-powered relocation concierge
- Residency application guidance
- Housing search
- Banking & finance setup
- Local services (vetted lawyers, accountants, healthcare)

**Pricing:** Not explicitly listed on landing page. Claims "traditional agencies charge thousands, Terravida works around the clock."

**Unique Selling Points:**
- **The only AI-first competitor in the market**
- 24/7 AI concierge
- Personalized relocation plan based on user goals
- Step-by-step guidance through every milestone
- Built by someone who lives in Paraguay
- Covers entire journey (residency → housing → banking → settlement)

**AI/Automation:** **Heavy AI use.** This is the primary differentiator. AI-powered relocation agent. This is the most direct AI competitor to Nexa Paraguay.

---

## 7. Other Notable Competitors
